name: Borda Berri
quote: where you can order hot dishes like a celebrated Idiazabal puntalette (made with the treasured local goat's milk cheese)
article_title: Eating the Basque Way in San Sebastián

name: Casa Urola
quote: a humble artichoke served with grilled Navarre cardoon, ham cream and salted almond praline feels like a warm hug
article_title: Eating the Basque Way in San Sebastián

name: La Cuchara de San Telmo
dishes_mentioned: foie a la plancha with apple marmalade
quote: the passage of time has not diminished the fame of its foie cooked on the griddle, served with apple marmalade
article_title: Eating the Basque Way in San Sebastián

name: La Viña
quote: Basque cheesecake is unique in that it's baked without a crust and often has a burnt/caramelized look to the outside.
article_title: San Sebastián Food Guide

name: Muka
dishes_mentioned: flores de calabacín rellenas de requesón
quote: Muka, que significa ceniza del fuego en euskera, es un restaurante que sí, gira en torno a la llama, pero que propone una experiencia desenfadada que busca que el comensal se lo pase bien
article_title: 24h sabrosas en San Sebastián

name: Ganbara
quote: something as simple as seasonal mushrooms, sautéed with oil and garlic and served with egg yolk, can leave you speechless
article_title: Eating the Basque Way in San Sebastián

name: Antonio Bar
dishes_mentioned: bonito con tomate y cebolla frita
quote: Three-table spot with perfect Spanish omelet, chalkboard menu of hot dishes, cured seafood and salads.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: La Espiga
quote: Opened in 1928, currently run by fourth generation, packed all day with locals and tourists.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Bar Txepetxa
quote: Classic bar devoted to tiny fish—famous for converting 99% of anchovy-haters since 1972—with vinegar-cured boquerón anchovies as the house specialty.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Casa Valles
quote: A frozen-in-time bar founded in 1942 in the city center, where the Gilda—an anchovy, olive, and pickled pepper skewer—is said to have been invented.
article_title: The Pintxo Bars I Can't Live Without in San Sebastián

name: Bar Nestor
quote: Known locally as 'the holy trinity' because it cooks three things to absolute perfection: tomato salad, Padrón peppers, and txuleta.
article_title: Where to Eat in San Sebastian: The Ultimate Food Guide

name: Bodegón Alejandro
quote: While not a Michelin star restaurant, it is managed by the famous Martín Berasategui and serves an unbeatable tasting menu.
article_title: Where to Eat in San Sebastian: The Ultimate Food Guide

name: La Mejillonera
quote: This unique and delicious 'mussel bar' is just that—a pintxos bar dedicated to serving mussels in different ways.
article_title: Where to Eat in San Sebastian: The Ultimate Food Guide

name: Txuleta
quote: Another great place for a steak is called (appropriately) Txuleta. Definitely make a reservation if you want to score a table.
article_title: Where to Eat in San Sebastian: The Ultimate Food Guide

name: Bar Haizea
quote: I wrote all about my love for Haizea here, and it hasn't changed. Classic pintxos, friendly service, and totally local.
article_title: Where to Eat in San Sebastian: The Ultimate Food Guide

name: Aratz
quote: One of the few emblematic asadores within city limits with gorgeous fresh fish and bone-in txuleta steaks.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Maun
quote: No-fuss restaurant stall dining on radically fresh produce right as it leaves the producer's hand.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Arzak
quote: The hub of fine dining in Spain, where the Basque cooking movement of the '70s, nueva cocina vasca, started.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Zazpi
quote: One of the finest places in the city for a luxurious long lunch, with a thoughtful seasonal tasting menu and beautiful natural light.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Akelarre
quote: Chef Pedro Subijana's haute cuisine strikes the perfect balance between innovative and comfort food, with floor-to-ceiling views of the Cantabrian Sea.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Narru
quote: A restaurant on an outdoor arcade overlooking the Buen Pastor cathedral, where locals see and are seen on weekend lunches.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: R Restaurante Bar
quote: An impressive new restaurant in Gros, laser-focused on in-season produce, with a creative menu served in a cozy, low-lit dining room.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Bistro Ondarreta
quote: A French bistro in Antiguo with tiny tables, retro black-and-white tiled floors, a curvy bar, and Gallic dishes including gougères and lobster pasta.
article_title: The 25 Best Restaurants In San Sebastián

name: Bar Urkabe
quote: This locals-only spot in Gros is known for chicken-studded croquetas made by simmering the bechamel in the same pan used to sear the breasts.
article_title: The Pintxo Bars I Can't Live Without in San Sebastián

name: Bar Martínez
dishes_mentioned: pimiento relleno de bonito del norte
bonito del norte tartar in piquillo pepper
quote: High-quality tinned bonito del norte (albacore) is flaked and folded into thick tartar sauce, then stuffed into a sweet, roasted, ruby-red piquillo pepper.
article_title: The Pintxo Bars I Can't Live Without in San Sebastián

name: Bar Bergara
quote: You'll only find La Txalupa at this legendary Gros bar, whose elaborate cold pintxos on display have made Bergara one of the city's most celebrated pintxo destinations.
article_title: The Pintxo Bars I Can't Live Without in San Sebastián

name: La Cepa
quote: With dark wood interiors and jamón legs hanging from the ceiling, eating a stuffed bell pepper at La Cepa is synonymous with pleasure.
article_title: Eating the Basque Way in San Sebastián

name: Bar Desy
quote: The tomato and tuna plate is a great tapas dish for sharing and they have some of the best bacon wrapped shrimp I've ever had!
article_title: San Sebastián Food Guide

name: Kbzon Haundi
quote: The biggest star here for us, though, was the charcoal duck carpaccio served lightly grilled, thinly sliced, and seasoned with sauce.
article_title: San Sebastián Food Guide

name: Aldapeta Gastrobar
quote: Aldapeta Gastrobar is surprisingly good for a hotel restaurant, with standout dishes like scallops with sea foam and braised octopus with kimchi sauce.
article_title: San Sebastián Food Guide
